disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset over about 6 hours of severe distress and inconsolable crying triggered specifically by opening/spreading the legs during nappy changes
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and what were you doing?
What seems to set off the crying?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started about six hours ago at a nappy change. Now every time I try to open her legs to clean her she screams like she's in agony.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New apparent left leg-length discrepancy noticed today (left leg looks shorter)
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed anything different about how her legs look?
Do her legs look the same length?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Today her left leg suddenly looks shorter than the right one, especially when I bend her knees up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced/absent spontaneous movement of the left leg over the past few hours
trigger_question_examples: Is she moving both legs normally?
Is she kicking that leg like usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's stopped kicking her left leg the way she normally would — she's barely moving it now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding uneven/asymmetric skin creases on the left buttock and thigh, noticed by the grandmother before today
trigger_question_examples: Have her skin folds or creases ever looked uneven?
Has anyone noticed anything about her hips or thighs before today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, her nan had said the creases on her left bottom and thigh looked deeper and uneven, but she seemed fine so we didn't worry about it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of hip problems in infancy on the mother's side
trigger_question_examples: Does anyone in the family have hip problems?
Any family history of hip trouble as babies?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — on my side a couple of us had something done to our hips when we were babies.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no redness or heat over the leg, and the leg looks a normal colour and is warm — screening negatives for joint infection and for limb ischaemia
trigger_question_examples: Does the leg look red, hot, pale or a different colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that I've measured — 37.1 on the forehead thermometer. The leg's a normal colour, warm, not red.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think she's hot but I'm not really sure how to tell with a baby.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No history of any fall, trauma or injury
trigger_question_examples: Has she had any fall, knock or injury?
Could anything have happened to that leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing happened — no falls, no knocks, she wasn't dropped or anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Deterioration screen: has she developed a fever, does the leg look pale/cold/mottled, or is she refusing all feeds? (currently negative — these are the escalation triggers)
trigger_question_examples: Has she become hot, or has the leg turned pale or cold?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's still feeding, maybe a bit less, but she hasn't refused completely. No fever and the leg isn't pale or cold.
